conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're responsible for things like paying for his food, clothing, and other needs.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after all his needs - his food, clothes, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

Just one more question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in local authority care, or has he been in hospital for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he has been in hospital actually. It's been a few months now - around 15 weeks I think. But I'm still regularly spending money on him - buying him things he needs, his favorite snacks, magazines, that sort of thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, I can now provide you with a decision on your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ He lives with you
✓ You are responsible for him
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

**Important note about Alex's hospital stay:** Although Alex has been in hospital for more than 12 weeks (around 15 weeks), you can still claim Child Benefit because you're regularly spending money on him - such as buying him the things he needs, snacks, and magazines. The UK government allows you to continue receiving Child Benefit for a child in hospital beyond 12 weeks if you're regularly spending money on them, which you are.

You should be able to make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. I hope this helps, and I wish Alex a speedy recovery!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you, and you are responsible for him. You live in the UK and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Although Alex has been in hospital for approximately 15 weeks (more than 12 weeks), you remain eligible for Child Benefit because you are regularly spending money on him. According to UK government rules, you can continue to get Child Benefit for a child in hospital for more than 12 weeks if you're regularly spending money on them, which you are doing by buying him things he needs, snacks, and magazines. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Despite Alex being in hospital for over 12 weeks, the claimant continues to regularly spend money on him, which means they can still claim Child Benefit under the hospital care exception.
